title: Spatula with channels for turning food
abstract: A spatula (1) having a head (5) with channels (11) that automatically turn or flip food, such as cubed meats, ground beef, vegetables and so forth, when the food travels through the channels.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  Having thus described my invention, I claim: 1. A spatula for use with preparing food comprising: a handle having a proximal end and a distal end; a head located on the distal end of the handle; and said head having substantially C-shaped channels located on opposing sides of the head. 2. The spatula of claim 1 further comprising: a wedge located on a front surface of the head. 3. The spatula of claim 1 further comprising: a rounded projection located within each of the substantially C-shaped channels. 4. The spatula of claim 1 further comprising: each of the substantially C-shaped channels curving over each of the rounded projections located therein. 5. T he spatula of claim 1 further comprising: a rear wall located on the head. 6. A spatula for use with preparing food comprising: a handle having a proximal end and a distal end; a head located on the distal end of the handle; said had having a bottom surface, a top surface, side surfaces, a rear surface and a front surface; a wedge located on the front surface of the head; and said head having a substantially C-shaped channel located on each of the side surfaces of the head. 7. Said spatula of claim 6 further comprising: at least one rounded projection located within each of the substantially C- shaped channels. 8. Said spatula of claim 6 further comprising: a rear wall located on the head. 9. Said spatula of claim 6 wherein: said wedge is formed by a triangular shape of the bottom surface. 10. Said spatula of claim 9 wherein: said wedge is formed by a triangular shape of the top surface. 11. A spatula for use with preparing food comprising: a handle having a proximal end and a distal end; a head located on the distal end of the handle; said had having a bottom surface, a top surface, side surfaces, a rear surface and a front surface; a wedge located on the front surface of the head; said head having a substantially C-shaped channel located on each of the side surfaces of the head; and at least one rounded projection located within each of the substantially C- shaped channels. 12. Said spatula of claim 11 further comprising: a rear wall located on the head. 13. Said spatula of claim 11 wherein: said wedge is formed by a triangular shape of the bottom surface. 14. Said spatula of claim 14 wherein: said wedge is formed by a triangular shape of the top surface.
